title: AFIS Search Workflow
description: Step through the five AFIS stations from latent scan to examiner review, with concrete data at each stage: the scanned print, the (x, y, angle) minutiae template, the database search, the scored candidate list, and the human examiner decision.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Fingerprint Analysis
Dactyloscopy
AFIS
Minutiae
bloomLevel: Understand (L2) — explain how AFIS encodes minutiae and generates a candidate list for examiner review.

educational.topic: AFIS fingerprint search pipeline and minutiae encoding
learningObjectives: Explain how AFIS encodes a fingerprint as a numeric minutiae template rather than an image.
Describe what an AFIS candidate list and similarity scores mean.
Explain why a human examiner, not AFIS, makes the final identification or exclusion.
educational.prerequisites: Familiarity with fingerprint ridge patterns and minutiae

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Workflow step-through: advance through five stations, click a station to reveal its description and a stage-specific data visualization
pedagogical.assessment: Students explain each stage and interpret the candidate scores and examiner role
pedagogical.modelType: Five-station pipeline with animated minutiae extraction and a scored candidate list
controls: click a station box to jump to that stage
